compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Epic execution start; plan-review LLM gate timed out >10min repeatedly on this epic, decision owner-ratified in ADR 0047 (#661). Documented force per CLAUDE.md gotcha.".

compiled_state.description:
## Context

Cost-driven POC simplification, decided 2026-07-13 after the auto-lander (epic f1fa) generated a sustained bug spiral (dc33 annotation-store [4 layers], 15a1 FF-string-match drive loop, 0d3e Autosubmit-dropped-on-rebase, plus the FFO rebase-treadmill re-CI cost). Root cause: the auto-lander is a stateful, polling, client-side bridge that re-implements Gerrit/git integration to reconcile Gerrit's synchronous submit gate with GitHub Actions' async CI **across a mandatory rebase** — and that mandatory rebase exists ONLY because of the Fast-Forward-Only submit type (ADR 0040). Gerrit's own submit types make the server do the integration: **Rebase-If-Necessary** rebases-and-submits server-side, atomically, under Gerrit's lock + ACL.

Decision (owner): (1) RELAX the pre-merge requirement to re-run full integration CI after a non-conflicted rebase — accept the bounded, rare semantic-conflict risk; (2) switch main FFO -> Rebase-If-Necessary so Gerrit integrates server-side; (3) DROP the auto-lander bot ENTIRELY (too expensive to maintain for too little value) — agents Submit directly when both votes are green; (4) rely on the EXISTING (free) post-merge main CI as the semantic-conflict detector, with MANUAL revert on the rare red (no auto-revert infra — cost constraint). Own CI infra (Zuul) is out (3x hosting cost, cannot use GitHub Actions runner). LUCI-CV-style batching only pays off at far larger scale.

Net effect: process becomes cheaper (no re-CI on rebases, no lander container/infra) AND more reliable (Gerrit owns the atomic rebase-submit; deletes the entire client-side bug surface: string-parsing, label-as-queue, polling TOCTOU, crash-recovery, container ticket-store). Supersedes the lander portions of ADR 0040 (FFO) and ADR 0042 (auto-lander).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ADR 0047 records the decision + supersedes lander parts of 0040/0042 (child: ADR story).
- [ ] main submit type is Rebase-If-Necessary on refs/meta/config; Autosubmit label + rebase-on-behalf ACL retired (child: cutover).
- [ ] All infra/autolander/ code, tests, the land/land-status command, and land-contract.md are deleted (child: retire-code).
- [ ] Deployed auto-lander infra decommissioned: container, compose service + gerrit_autolander* volumes, autodeploy hooks, fetch-secrets autolander token, terraform (ssm.tf autolander param + monitoring_autolander.tf), nginx /autolander/status route, SSM /rebar/prod/autolander-gerrit-token, CloudWatch rebar-autolander-errors alarm (child: decommission-infra).
- [ ] Landing docs (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/your-first-change.md, README.md) rewritten: 'Submit via Gerrit when LLM-Review+1 and Verified+1; Gerrit rebases server-side; on a textual conflict rebase manually; post-merge main CI + manual revert is the safety net' (child: docs).
- [ ] Post-merge safety net documented: existing main-branch CI as detector + a manual-revert runbook (child: safety-net).
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green after code/infra deletion.

## Context / resumability
Every workstream is a child story/task below with its own AC. Auto-lander bugs dc33/15a1/0d3e are OBSOLETED by retire-code (deleting the code IS their resolution) — linked here; close them when retire-code lands. 85c3 (CI flakes) is INDEPENDENT and still valuable (agents' own Submits still need a reliable Verified) — NOT superseded. Prior epic f1fa is superseded (commented there). Sequencing: ADR first (design record) -> retire-code + decommission-infra + docs (can parallelize) -> cutover (the pivotal refs/meta/config flip, operator apply in a quiet window, done LAST so nothing depends on the removed bits mid-flight; back-out is a one-line submit-type revert). Landing is manual Gerrit Submit throughout (the bot is paused/being removed).
compiled_state.title: Retire the auto-lander; land via Gerrit Rebase-If-Necessary + agent Submit
